Start a build
Trading infrastructure

SMC SuperFIB

SuperFIB is a distributed system that synchronises indicator logic, a PHP and WordPress API, MT5 expert advisors, and a React dashboard. The design priority is not novelty. It is keeping every part aligned around one authoritative state.

Primary challenge Cross-runtime parity

Different surfaces needed to agree on state, timing, and visibility without leaving operators to guess which layer was right.

The API sits at the center so execution clients and the dashboard can reconcile against the same reality.

What I solved
  • Backend-as-source-of-truth behavior across several runtimes.
  • Broker time normalization and nearest-minute handling to reduce jitter.
  • Staleness gates and hold states so risky inputs could be blocked cleanly.
  • Parity audits to catch drift between indicator output, execution state, and dashboard presentation.
Why the architecture mattered

Without an authoritative backend, the different clients would eventually disagree. That is unacceptable in a trading context.

What the user sees

The dashboard is designed to make system state understandable quickly, not just visually appealing.

What stays private

I can show the topology and the hardening approach without exposing trade logic, formulas, or edge thresholds.

SS Developers

I build trading, fintech, and data-heavy systems for teams that need the software to stay correct when the environment gets messy.
